jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer - Cloud
jobs.summary: Are you passionate about technology and interested in joining a community of collaborative colleagues who respectfully and courageously seek to challenge the status quo? If so, read on to learn more about an exciting opportunity with Deloitte's Technology...
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about technology and interested in joining a community of collaborative colleagues who respectfully and courageously seek to challenge the status quo? If so, read on to learn more about an exciting opportunity with Deloitte's Technology & Infrastructure (T&I) team. We are insatiably curious and life-long learners focused on technology and innovation.

Recruiting for this role ends on 7/28/2026.

Work you'll do

Responsible for implementation, management, cloud security and automation of the GovCloud for Deloitte GPS channel hosted in Hub and Spoke model. The Cloud Engineer will work within Deloitte Cloud Framework to implement security controls and works with multiple Deloitte internal engagement teams to gather technology requirements, define cloud architecture, implement policies, standards, security, and establish best practices and deliver technical expertise in cloud management to meet the engagement team expectations. Provides suggestions and recommendations for new technology solutions; Automates reoccurring tasks using scripting tools. Collaborates with Senior Leadership and multiple departments delivering on major national Firm Information Technologies initiatives. May serve internal or external clients.

The successful candidate will:

Review proposed application/solution architecture for newly onboarded products.Ensure that all cloud solutions follow internally defined security and compliance controls.Create, implement, and assist in the development of scripts to automate everyday operations.Assist with major IT environment upgrades, enhancements, new functionality, capacities, performance, cost effectiveness, and business integrations.Analyzes and resolves technology and end user incidents during standard business hours or on-call rotations; Monitors issue resolutions and collaborates with the engagement teams and engineers when necessary to resolve and apply fixes. Identify root causes, document problems, and implement preventive measures.Assists with special projects and initiatives such as the ITS CTO annual project; Plans and execute on major milestones.Provides input and recommendations on new potential technology solutions based on research and analysis.Maintains standard operations procedure documentation.Performs other job-related duties as assigned.Participates in the on-call rotation providing 24x7 support.Hands on experience providing day-to-day support for GCP services, including Compute Engine (VMs), Cloud Storage, IAM, and some Networking (firewall troubleshooting).Continuously build GCP platform knowledge and apply learned capabilities to strengthen the offering over time.Ability to work independently and collaborate as part of a team Effective written and verbal communication skills Meticulous attention to detail and quality of work product Ability to build and sustain professional relationships Ability to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a fast-paced and dynamic environment Strong interpersonal skills and professional demeanor Ability to meet deadlines Ability to provide clear guidance to others

The successful candidate would possess these skills

Ability to work independently and collaborate as part of a teamEffective written and verbal communication skillsMeticulous attention to detail and quality of work productAbility to build and sustain professional relationshipsAbility to lead projects or workstreamsAbility to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a fast-paced and dynamic environmentStrong interpersonal skills and professional demeanorAbility to meet deadlinesAbility to provide clear guidance to others

The Team

The Deloitte GPS TC Team provides a FedRAMP High-compliant cloud platform and infrastructure, enabling client engagement teams to build and deploy critical solutions. We support and manage infrastructure up to the application layer in a heavily STIG-ed environment that is continually audited and monitored to maintain the ISO 27001, 27017, and 22301 certifications. By partnering closely with Cyber Security, we ensure strict compliance is maintained alongside application availability and uptime and aim for successful audits of our security controls. We offer customized multi-cloud support across AWS, Azure, and GCP, empowering our clients to launch cutting-edge internal and go-to-market solutions on the platform best suited for their mission.

The GPS TC Team delivers services internally including:

Network ManagementIncident Management (Following ITIL Model)Server DeploymentFirewall SupportTechnology & InfrastructureCompliance & Security StandardsPatch ManagementLayer 1 security guardrails across all Cloud Service ProvidersIdentity Access ManagementCommunications

Qualifications

Required:

Bachelor's Degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field.Minimum 5 years of experience supporting IT Infrastructure environments.Implement security controls, enforce policies, and review proposed architectures for newly onboarded products to ensure compliance.Write and implement scripts to automate daily operations and assist with IT upgrades, performance tuning, and business integrations.Resolve technology/user incidents, identify root causes, and implement preventative measures while maintaining standard operating procedures.

Preferred:

Experience with automating tasks using scripting tools. (PowerShell, Terraform , GCP Deployment manager, etc.)Knowledge of modern compute technologies, containers, serverless and PaaS technologies.Hands-on experience with designing and deploying Cloud Services (IaaS, PaaS and SaaS)Knowledge of Cloud networking and routing and traditional network, firewalls and load balancers, and hub-spoke models.Knowledge of the design and deployment of dynamically scalable fault-tolerant, reliable, and highly available applications.Understanding of the Federal Government compliance requirements a plus.Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present and collaborate with business leaders.Knowledge of information technology and enterprise architecture best practicesExperience working in a team-oriented, collaborative environmentGoogle Cloud Certification - Associate Cloud Engineer (ACE)

The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $93,000 to $191,000.

You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance.

jobs.title: Principal Infrastructure Engineer
jobs.summary: Consequential Work. Dedicated People. About Onebrief Onebrief builds collaboration and AI-powered workflow software for military planning and operational coordination. Today, many critical planning workflows still rely on fragmented systems, static...
jobs.description:
Consequential Work. Dedicated People.
About Onebrief
Onebrief builds collaboration and AI-powered workflow software for military planning and operational coordination.

Today, many critical planning workflows still rely on fragmented systems, static documents, and disconnected tools that make collaboration and decision-making unnecessarily difficult. Onebrief brings modern software, AI, and real-time collaboration into those environments, helping teams operate with greater clarity, coordination, and adaptability in situations where decisions carry real-world consequences.

We are a distributed team of builders from military, operational, and technology backgrounds who care deeply about improving how important work gets done. Some team members work remotely, while others work directly alongside customers in operational environments around the world.

Founded in 2019, Onebrief is backed by leading investors including General Catalyst, Battery Ventures, Insight Partners, Sapphire Ventures, and Human Capital. Valued at more than $2 billion, we continue to invest in product innovation, AI capabilities, and team growth.

About the RoleOnebrief's infrastructure team owns the platform that delivers our software to military staffs. Our charter spans Kubernetes clusters in commercial and government cloud, application streaming for thin-client users on classified networks, a unified artifact pipeline that signs and ships the same builds to every environment we operate, and air-gapped appliances at forward sites.

We're hiring an Infrastructure Engineer who builds security into the systems they operate rather than treating it as a separate workstream. You'll architect, build, and run the platform end to end. This includes RFCs, PRDs, and reviewing and writing production code alongside Product and Engineering teammates. The work cuts across cloud-native deployments, edge appliances, data platforms, and the application streaming layer that puts Onebrief on classified environments. You'll partner with Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) and mission owners on STIGs, CVE remediation, audit prep, and the controls work that lets us operate in environments such as IL5, IL6, and JWICS.

Success means a platform that stays up under real operational load, ships trusted artifacts to every profile we run, and passes audits without fire drills.

About YouPeople by-name request you because you bias towards action. You are a problem-solver by nature who genuinely cares about the mission and the outcome. You operate without hand-holding. You read a problem, weigh tradeoffs, and move. Sometimes the tradeoffs have harsh consequences that you pay for later, but you are resilient and adapt. When the work crosses into expertise you don't have, you dig into the problem, pull in the right teammates, apply their feedback, and continue.

What You'll DoDesign, develop, test, iterate, and deploy secure production systems across cloud-native and edge appliance deployments

Embed with cross-functional teams and advise on infrastructure, security, and deployment best practices that hold up in production

Own end-to-end infrastructure outcomes for one or more critical programs or priority infrastructure initiatives

Harden the artifact pipeline so the same signed builds run in cloud, single-tenant high-side, and air-gapped appliance deployments

What We Look ForYou'll do well in this role if you bring:

8+ years operating production infrastructure in DevOps/DevSecOps, Platform, SRE, Cloud, or Infrastructure role

Full-stack engineering experience when necessary, e.g., production experience designing, building, and operating services in a modern backend language (e.g., Go, Python, Rust, TypeScript)

Deep production Kubernetes experience: operators, networking, storage, multi-cluster operations, and a feel for what breaks at scale

Expertise with major cloud providers (e.g., Amazon AWS, Microsoft Azure), with strong instincts for designing across providers and profiles

Strong application and infrastructure security fundamentals: identity, network segmentation, secrets management, common vulnerability classes, and sound security judgment under ambiguity

Demonstrated ability to build working solutions from scratch, connect disparate applications together, and jump into existing code bases to add value

Experience packaging and shipping workloads into disconnected or air-gapped environments

Demonstrated leadership/mentorship experience

Ability to obtain a SECRET clearance

Nice to have qualifications:

Active US SECRET or TOP SECRET security clearance

Government cloud expertise (AWS GovCloud, Azure Government)

FedRAMP, IL4/IL5, IL6, or JWICS environment exposure

Audit experience (FedRAMP, SOC 2, or equivalent)

Big data and distributed data experience

Prior experience supporting defense, intelligence, or other regulated industries

Notice to Third Party Recruitment Agencies
Please note that Onebrief does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters or employment agencies. In the absence of an executed Recruitment Services Agreement, there will be no obligation to any referral compensation or recruiter fee. In the event a recruiter or agency submits a resume or candidate without an agreement Onebrief explicitly reserves the right to pursue and hire those candidate(s) without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. Any unsolicited resumes, including those submitted to hiring managers, shall be deemed the property of Onebrief.

jobs.title: Senior Systems Engineer, End-User Computing
jobs.summary: At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience. Delta is focused on sustaining a...
jobs.description:
At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.
Delta is focused on sustaining a strong IT operation, growing our capabilities, and maximizing optimization across each of our tech hubs to elevate the travel experience for our customers and empower our 90,000 Delta people. We’re committed to fostering innovation, and we’re excited to invite you to be part of our journey as we shape the future of technology at the world’s best airline!
A Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is an experienced technical specialist responsible for the architecture, deployment, support, and continuous improvement of secure, efficient end-user computing environments across an organization. This role covers a broad spectrum of EUC technologies, including desktop virtualization, device management, security, application delivery, and end-user support, ensuring optimal user experience and productivity. Senior EUC Engineers often serve as technical leads, mentoring junior staff and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver seamless digital workspaces.
Key Responsibilities EUC Architecture and Design: Develop and maintain strategic EUC infrastructure designs, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with business needs. Evaluate, recommend, and implement new technologies and solutions. Technical Implementation and Support: Lead the deployment, configuration, and maintenance of EUC solutions such as VDI (Virtual Desktop Infrastructure), application packaging, and endpoint security platforms. Incident Management and Troubleshooting: Serve as an escalation point for complex technical issues, diagnosing and resolving incidents related to desktops, laptops, virtual machines, and application delivery. Automation and Scripting: Develop and maintain automation scripts and tools to streamline EUC operations, increase efficiency, and reduce manual intervention. Security and Compliance: Implement and enforce policies for device and data security, ensuring compliance with organizational standards and regulatory requirements. Participate in security audits and remediation. Collaboration and Leadership: Work closely with IT leadership, service desk, infrastructure, and security teams. Mentor junior engineers and contribute to technical knowledge sharing and documentation. Performance Optimization: Monitor EUC environments for performance, capacity, and availability, proactively identifying improvements or upgrades to maximize user satisfaction. Project Management: Lead or participate in EUC-related projects, from requirements gathering through design, implementation, and post-implementation support. Provide status updates and documentation throughout the project lifecycle. End-User Experience: Partner with business units to understand user requirements and deliver solutions that enhance productivity and usability, ensuring minimal disruption during change or migration activities. Documentation and Reporting: Maintain comprehensive technical documentation, configurations, and procedures for EUC infrastructure and services. Generate regular reports on system health, incidents, and improvement initiatives.

jobs.title: Senior End-User Computing Engineer
jobs.summary: Overview The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for overseeing the organization's end-user computing environment, ensuring the efficient operation, security, and optimization of desktop, laptop, and virtual desktop infrastructure...
jobs.description:
Overview

The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer is responsible for overseeing the organization's end-user computing environment, ensuring the efficient operation, security, and optimization of desktop, laptop, and virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI). This role involves advanced troubleshooting, leadership in project management, and collaborating with IT teams on technology strategy and integration. The Senior End-User Computing (EUC) Engineer will act as a key technical expert in designing and implementing complex desktop solutions across the enterprise. Pay range for the role is $140,000 to $150,000, with an annual bonus. The role can be 100% remote anywhere in United States.

Responsibilities

Advanced Desktop Infrastructure Management:Architect, deploy and maintain the organization's desktop environments, including Windows-based physical and virtual desktops (VDI).Develop and manage automated desktop deployments using tools such as Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) or equivalent technologies.Design, implement, and maintain desktop images, ensuring they are optimized for performance and security.Leadership and Strategy:Lead desktop infrastructure projects, such as OS migrations, VDI implementations, and major software rollouts.Provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior engineers and support staff.Collaborate with senior IT leadership to develop and implement long-term desktop technology strategies.Security and Compliance:Ensure compliance with internal security policies, industry standards, and audit requirements.Lead initiatives to enhance desktop security, including endpoint protection, encryption, and vulnerability management.Manage group policies, endpoint security software, and patch management processes using WSUS or other tools.Troubleshooting and Escalation:Serve as the highest escalation point for complex desktop-related issues, providing Level 3 support.Perform in-depth root cause analysis for recurring or complex technical problems and implement preventative measures.Provide expert support for remote access solutions, VPNs, and cloud desktop services.Automation and Efficiency:Develop and implement automation scripts using PowerShell and other tools to streamline desktop management tasks.Continuously seek opportunities to improve end-user computing efficiency, including desktop performance tuning and reducing downtime.System Integration:Collaborate with server, network, and security teams to ensure the seamless integration of desktop systems into the broader IT infrastructure.Ensure optimal configuration and performance of desktop environments within cloud services like Microsoft 365 and Azure Active Directory.Manage and integrate virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware Horizon, Citrix, Hyper-V).Documentation and Knowledge Sharing:Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for desktop systems, policies, and procedures.Ensure knowledge sharing and training for IT staff on new technologies, best practices, and troubleshooting techniques.Actively participate in developing and updating standard operating procedures (SOPs) for desktop support.
Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent experience).5+ years of experience in desktop engineering or support, with significant experience in enterprise-level desktop management.Demonstrated experience leading major desktop infrastructure projects, including large-scale deployments, migrations, and VDI implementation.Expertise in Microsoft technologies, including SCCM, Intune, and Active Directory.Advanced knowledge of Windows 10/11, Group Policy, and desktop management tools.Strong PowerShell scripting skills for automation and troubleshooting.Experience with virtualization technologies (e.g., VMware, Citrix, Hyper-V) and virtual desktop environments (VDI).Familiarity with cloud-based services and integration (Microsoft 365, Azure AD, etc.).Strong leadership and team collaboration skills.Ability to manage multiple priorities and projects in a fast-paced environment.Excellent problem-solving and critical thinking abilities.Outstanding communication skills, both written and verbal.Certifications, Licenses, and/or RegistrationMicrosoft Certified: Modern Desktop Administrator ExpertMicrosoft Certified: Azure Fundamentals or Administrator AssociateVMware VCP or Citrix certifications.ITIL Certification or other relevant service management certifications.Physical Demands and Work EnvironmentThe physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit and use hands to handle, touch or feel objects, tools, or controls. The employee frequently is required to talk and hear. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate. The employee is occasionally required to stand; walk; reach with hands and arms. The employee is rarely required to stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, and the ability to adjust focus.

All aspects of consideration for employment and employment with the Company are governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by federal, state, or local law.

jobs.title: Digital Workplace Service Engineer
jobs.summary: Job Description: Overview: We don’t simply hire employees. We invest in them. When you work at Chatham, we empower you — offering professional development opportunities to help you grow in your career, no matter if you've been here for five months or 15...
jobs.description: Job Description: Overview: We don’t simply hire employees. We invest in them. When you work at Chatham, we empower you — offering professional development opportunities to help you grow in your career, no matter if you've been here for five months or 15 years. Chatham has worked hard to create a distinct work environment that values people, teamwork, integrity, and client service. You will have immediate opportunities to partner with talented subject matter experts, work on complex projects, and contribute to the value Chatham delivers every day. The Digital Workplace Engineer is part of the Digital Workplace Services team within Chatham’s IT organization, focused on creating a modern, seamless technology experience for employees across the firm. The team supports all internal users, empowering better communication and collaboration through tools like Microsoft 365, Teams, Sharepoint, and AI-driven solutions. In this individual contributor role, you’ll act as both a technology champion and trusted advisor—driving adoption of new tools, sharing best practices, and helping employees unlock the full value of the firm’s workplace technologies. In this role you will: Enthusiastically promote advanced technology solutions to employees, ensuring they understand and appreciate the benefits and usefulness of our end-user tech through various media, including articles, webinars, videos, in-person events, and more. Actively engage with vendors and the tech community to leverage best practices, learn industry trends, and stay ahead of technological advancements. jobs.description:
About the position

As a Digital Workplace Engineering Lead you will be a hands‑on technical leader responsible for the operational excellence, design, implementation and optimization of enterprise digital workplace solutions. You’ll apply deep technical expertise across one or more workplace domains while providing technical leadership, mentoring, and strategic influence across teams and stakeholders.

Responsibilities

Drive operational excellence You will lead complex incident response and post‑mortems; implement corrective actions to prevent recurrence. You will champion automation, monitoring, and standardized engineering practices. You will partner cross‑functionally to improve reliability, cost effectiveness, and speed to delivery.Design and engineer secure, reliable services You will design, implement, and optimize scalable, reliable, secure, compliant workplace services. You will review and influence technical designs to meet best practices, performance standards, and security requirements. You will use service health metrics to remove bottlenecks and improve resilience and scalability.Lead technology strategy and roadmaps You will serve as a subject matter expert and provide design guidance across initiatives. You will drive technology strategy including lifecycle management, roadmap planning, adoption, and continuous improvement. You will research industry trends and emerging technologies to shape future-state capabilities.Collaborate and influence You will partner with business leaders, IT, security, compliance, and office services to deliver integrated solutions. You will communicate technical risks, tradeoffs, and recommendations clearly to senior leadership. You will influence standardized technologies and enterprise engineering practices across teams.Mentor and uplift the team You will provide coaching and mentorship; foster a culture of knowledge sharing. You will support hiring, onboarding, and development of new associates. You will model innovation, collaboration, continuous improvement, and customer focus.Requirements

competitive salarybonuses and benefitscompany-funded retirement contributiongenerous time-away and health benefits from day one, with the opportunity for flexible work options2-for-1 matching gifts for your charitable contributions and the opportunity to secure annual grants for the organizations you loveon-demand professional development resources that allow you to hone existing skills and learn new ones

SRI Tech Solutions Inc., an IT services and consulting firm with a focus on staffing, project solutions, and product development. Job Title: Digital Workplace Engineer
Location: Kingston, NC

This role is responsible for the design, deployment, management, and support of all end-user computing devices, collaboration tools, and the overall digital employee experience. The ideal candidate will possess a strong foundation in end-user technology coupled with a solid understanding of networking principles, IT infrastructure components, and cybersecurity best practices. You will play a crucial role in ensuring the stability, security, and efficient operation of our digital workplace environment while collaborating closely with network, infrastructure, and security teams to enhance productivity and user satisfaction. You will also have to support the users adjust and adapt to the new solutions that will be deployed over the coming months. Key Responsibilities:

Digital Workplace Technology Management:Image, configure, and deploy new and replacement desktop, laptop, and mobile systems.Manage and maintain endpoint management solutions (e.g., SCCM, Intune, Jamf) for software distribution, patch management, inventory, and policy enforcement.Administer and support collaboration platforms (e.g., Microsoft 365, Teams, SharePoint, video conferencing solutions).Develop and maintain standard operating procedures (SOPs) for digital workplace support, deployment, and management.Network & Infrastructure Understanding:Troubleshoot basic network connectivity issues (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, Wi-Fi) impacting end-user devices and digital workplace services.Understand network topologies, VPN configurations, and cloud connectivity to assist in resolving user access problems.Collaborate with network and infrastructure teams on projects and incident resolution impacting the digital workplace and end-user services.Assist in documenting network and infrastructure configurations relevant to digital workplace support.Cybersecurity Focus:Implement and enforce endpoint security policies and configurations (e.g., antivirus, anti-malware, encryption, DLP, MFA) across the digital workplace.Respond to and assist in the remediation of security incidents involving end-user devices and accounts.Educate users on cybersecurity best practices and promote a security-aware culture.Ensure compliance with industry regulations (e.g., aerospace-specific, CMMC if applicable) and company security standards for all digital workplace technologies.Documentation & Reporting:Maintain accurate records of hardware inventory, software licenses, and support activities in the IT service management (ITSM) system.Create and update technical documentation, user guides, and knowledge base articles for digital workplace technologies.Generate reports on digital workplace performance, user satisfaction, and support metrics.Project & Collaboration:Participate in IT projects related to digital workplace upgrades, migrations, new technology implementations, and employee experience initiatives.Work collaboratively with other IT team members, business units, and vendors to deliver effective digital workplace solutions.Champion the adoption of new digital tools and practices to improve productivity and collaboration.
Education & Experience:Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field, OR equivalent practical experience.7 years of experience in a desktop support, endpoint engineering, or digital workplace role, preferably within a large enterprise or manufacturing environment.Technical Skills:
Proficiency in supporting and troubleshooting Windows (10/11) and macOS operating systems. iOS and Android experience is a plus.
Experience with endpoint management tools (e.g., Microsoft Intune, SCCM, Jamf).
Strong understanding of Active Directory, Azure AD, Group Policy, and identity and access management (IAM) concepts.
Experience with Microsoft 365 suite administration (Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, Exchange Online).Networking: Solid understanding of TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPN, and wireless networking concepts. Ability to perform basic network troubleshooting.Infrastructure: Familiarity with server virtualization (e.g., VMware, Hyper-V), cloud services (e.g., Azure, AWS), and general IT infrastructure components.Cybersecurity: Knowledge of endpoint security principles, antivirus/anti-malware solutions, data encryption, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and incident response basics. Familiarity with security frameworks (e.g., NIST, ISO 27001, CMMC) is a plus.Experience with scripting (e.g., PowerShell, Bash) for automation of digital workplace tasks is desirable.Familiarity with ITIL or other ITSM frameworks.Soft Skills:
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
Strong communication (written and verbal) and interpersonal skills, with an ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users.
Customer-focused with a dedication to providing a high-quality digital employee experience.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a collaborative environment.
Proactive, self-motivated, and eager to learn new technologies and improve processes.

jobs.description:
Job Description:

Overview:

The Digital Workplace Engineer is part of the Digital Workplace Services team within Chatham's IT organization, focused on creating a modern, seamless technology experience for employees across the firm. The team supports all internal users, empowering better communication and collaboration through tools like Microsoft 365, Teams, Sharepoint, and AI-driven solutions. In this individual contributor role, you'll act as both a technology champion and trusted advisor-driving adoption of new tools, sharing best practices, and helping employees unlock the full value of the firm's workplace technologies.

In this role you will:

Enthusiastically promote advanced technology solutions to employees, ensuring they understand and appreciate the benefits and usefulness of our end-user tech through various media, including articles, webinars, videos, in-person events, and more.Actively engage with vendors and the tech community to leverage best practices, learn industry trends, and stay ahead of technological advancements.Provide insightful user feedback to IT leadership to drive strategic product and engineering decisions.Partner with IT to lead the development of standards and best practices, serving as the authoritative voice of our end users.Manage end-user satisfaction and the adoption of M365, Teams, conference room equipment, digital signage, IPTV services, and telephony services.Lead governance, standards, cost models/chargeback, and citizen development within the Microsoft 365 suite.Take responsibility for key performance indicators to drive end-user satisfaction and engagement with technology.Lead engagements with the business to understand challenges and propose solutions for process improvement and business process automation.

Your impact:

Serve as a technical leader, advocating for users to deliver products and services that meet their needsLead innovation within the company by providing the appropriate tools and training to enable better communication and collaborationHelp drive business process automation through consulting and by delivering tools and guidelines for self-serviceBe a key contributor to developing IT's vision and roadmap for workplace productivity technology

Contributors to your success:

More than 5 years of experience in a similar role with a proven track record of successfully leading technology adoption and major technology deployments.Exceptional communication skills with the ability to gather requirements from and provide IT training to non-technical employees.Deep expertise in the Microsoft Office 365 suite of products.Demonstrated ability to solve complex problems through automation, preferably with Microsoft's Power Platform.Strong emphasis on customer service and the ability to teach people of all technical abilities.Experience working as a technical lead on major technology deployment and adoption efforts.Experience with Lean process improvement is a plus

For Denver based candidates, the compensation range for the position is expected to be between $82,000 and $100,000 annually. Total compensation, including base pay, discretionary individual bonus and company bonus, may be higher than range listed, depending on applicant's skills, qualifications, and experience. Benefits include health insurance, life and disability insurance, 401k, EAP, paid holidays and paid time off.

jobs.title: Modern Workplace Engineer
jobs.summary: The Modern Workplace Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, and supporting a secure, cloud-first end-user computing environment built on Microsoft technologies. This role focuses on Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Azure, and Microsoft...
jobs.description:
The Modern Workplace Engineer is responsible for designing, implementing, and supporting a secure, cloud-first end-user computing environment built on Microsoft technologies. This role focuses on Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Azure, and Microsoft Entra ID to deliver a modern, scalable, and user-centric workplace experience across Windows, mobile, and cloud platforms. The engineer partners closely with security, infrastructure, and end-user support teams to standardize device management, strengthen identity and access controls, and ensure seamless, secure productivity. The position plays a key role in advancing Zero Trust principles, improving operational efficiency, and enabling continuous improvement of the organization's modern workplace capabilities.

REPONSIBILITIES:

Design, implement, and operate Microsoft Intune and Windows Autopilot for device provisioning, configuration, compliance, patching, and application deployment across Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android platforms.Administer Microsoft Entra ID, including users, groups, devices, RBAC, MFA, SSO, and Conditional Access policies aligned with Zero Trust and secure access to Microsoft 365 and third-party applications.Implement endpoint security baselines, support Defender and compliance initiatives, ensure regulatory alignment, and participate in audits, incident response, and continuous security improvements.Support Azure-based services that enable the modern workplace, collaborate on hybrid and cloud-native identity/device strategies, and monitor configurations for performance, security, and cost optimization.Provide advanced troubleshooting for modern workplace issues, partner with service desk and engineering teams, and resolve complex endpoint, identity, and access-related incidents.Develop and maintain technical documentation, standards, and runbooks; evaluate new Microsoft workplace features; and recommend enhancements to improve user experience and operational efficiency.

REQUIREMENTS:

Bachelor's degree in Information Technology or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and hands-on enterprise experience supporting modern workplace technologies.Hands-on enterprise experience with Microsoft Intune for device and application managementProven experience deploying and managing Windows Autopilot for device provisioning and lifecycle managementStrong working knowledge of Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), including users, groups, devices, and Conditional AccessExperience supporting Microsoft Azure and cloud-based workplace servicesDeep understanding of Windows 11, modern device management, and endpoint security conceptsStrong troubleshooting, documentation, and communication skills in an enterprise environmentMicrosoft certifications such as MD-102, SC-300, AZ-104, or MS-102 are preferred

COMPENSATION: The national average salary range is $120,000-$130,000. The salary may be higher or lower depending on internal equity and years of experience.

BENEFITS: Medical Insurance, Dental Insurance, Vision Insurance, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Accounts, Life insurance, Disability Insurance, Paid Parental Leave, 401(k) Plan, Paid Days Off/Vacation, Firm Paid Holidays.

jobs.description:
About the position

The Modern Workplace Engineer Level I engineer supports Microsoft 365 environments for our Defense Industrial Base customers. This individual is responsible for delivering secure, compliant, and efficient modern workplace experiences using the Microsoft 365 E5 suite. This role will focus on both the ongoing operations of managed services as well as project-based onboarding and adoption. Role & Responsibilities: End User Productivity Support Provide Tier I support for Microsoft 365 applications, including: Exchange Online Microsoft Teams SharePoint Online OneDrive for Business Microsoft Office Apps (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.) Escalate support requests as necessary to Level II engineers. Troubleshoot issues related to Microsoft 365 access, synchronization, and collaboration. Identity & Access Support Configure and maintain Conditional Access, passwordless authentication, and identity protection policies in Microsoft Entra ID. Support hybrid identity scenarios and secure guest access across Microsoft 365 services. Security & Compliance Tasks Assist with reviewing and applying sensitivity labels, retention policies, and data loss prevention (DLP) rules within Microsoft Purview (Compliance Center). Monitor alerts in Microsoft Defender for Office 365 and escalate issues per defined procedures. Service Delivery & Operations Respond to service requests and incidents in alignment with SLAs and internal escalation paths. Perform basic configuration and policy updates using Microsoft 365 Admin Center and Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune). Follow internal processes (such as change controls) for making changes in customer environments. Document technical issues, solutions, and recurring patterns in internal knowledge base systems. Customer Engagement Work directly with customer stakeholders, primarily technical contacts, to resolve routine issues and implement M365 changes. Participate in onboarding activities for new users, sites, or departments within existing managed customers. Team Collaboration Coordinate with higher-level engineers to escalate complex issues or contribute to larger projects. Participate in regular team syncs and ongoing training sessions to stay current on Microsoft 365 features and changes. Coordinate with Endpoint Engineering, Security Operations, and Azure Engineering teams to resolve issues.

Responsibilities

End User Productivity Support Provide Tier I support for Microsoft 365 applicationsIdentity & Access Support Configure and maintain Conditional Access, passwordless authentication, and identity protection policies in Microsoft Entra ID.Security & Compliance Tasks Assist with reviewing and applying sensitivity labels, retention policies, and data loss prevention (DLP) rules within Microsoft Purview (Compliance Center).Service Delivery & Operations Respond to service requests and incidents in alignment with SLAs and internal escalation paths.Customer Engagement Work directly with customer stakeholders, primarily technical contacts, to resolve routine issues and implement M365 changes.Team Collaboration Coordinate with higher-level engineers to escalate complex issues or contribute to larger projects.Requirements

1–2 years of IT support experience, preferably with a managed service provider (MSP) or in a customer-facing technical role.Working knowledge of Microsoft 365 administration portals (Admin Center, Teams Admin Center, Exchange Admin Center).Strong troubleshooting skills and attention to detail.Excellent written and verbal communication skills.Ability to work independently while following defined processes and procedures administering Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Exchange Online, Microsoft Teams, Microsoft 365 Apps, sensitivity labels, Microsoft 365 Defender, and Active Directory.Familiarity with remote management and monitoring (RMM) tools and patch management tools.U.S. Citizenship required (due to work with defense contractors and ITAR-regulated customers).Nice-to-haves

jobs.title: Engineering - Workplace Engineering - Cloud Platform - M365 and Mobile Lead Engi
jobs.summary: Your Impact At Goldman Sachs, Workplace Engineering is at the forefront of driving our firm's adoption of cutting-edge technologies for productivity, communications, and collaboration. As a Senior Technical Vice President, you will play a pivotal...
jobs.description:
Your Impact

At Goldman Sachs, Workplace Engineering is at the forefront of driving our firm's adoption of cutting-edge technologies for productivity, communications, and collaboration. As a Senior Technical Vice President, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping the strategic direction, architecture, and operational excellence of our Microsoft 365 platforms. Your vision and leadership will directly influence how our global workforce interacts, collaborates, and innovates, ensuring robust, secure, and scalable solutions that underpin our business objectives and client engagement. You will lead a high-performing engineering organization, driving innovation in web, mobile, social, collaboration, and user experience, fundamentally changing the way we operate across the firm.

Role Summary

This role is a Microsoft 365 Engineer with a core focus on Microsoft 365 engineering, Intune (mobile application and device management), M365 platform readiness, extensibility, security, and enterprise-scale adoption. We are seeking a visionary and experienced technical leader to serve as our Senior Technical Vice President, overseeing the strategic planning, engineering, and governance of our enterprise-wide Microsoft 365 ecosystem. This role demands a deep technical understanding of M365 services, coupled with exceptional leadership capabilities to guide global teams, define architectural standards, and drive the adoption of advanced features, including AI-powered capabilities like Copilot, the Teams suite, and the Power Platform. You will be instrumental in collaborating with senior product owners, architects, compliance, and risk teams to deliver transformative solutions that meet the stringent demands of a global financial institution, ensuring seamless integration across the broader Microsoft ecosystem including Windows 365.

Responsibilities

* Define and execute the long-term strategic vision and roadmap for all Microsoft 365 platforms and services, ensuring alignment with overall firm objectives, technological advancements, and regulatory requirements.

* Lead, mentor, and develop a global team of highly skilled engineers and technical managers, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement.

* Provide executive oversight for the design, architecture, and implementation of complex, enterprise-grade Microsoft 365 solutions, ensuring scalability, security, resilience, and seamless integration with existing firm-wide systems, including Windows 365 and the Power Platform.

* Establish and enforce robust governance frameworks, security policies, and compliance standards for all Microsoft 365 services, ensuring adherence to internal policies and external regulatory mandates within a highly regulated financial services environment.

* Collaborate extensively with senior product owners, enterprise architects, engineering leads, compliance, risk, and business unit leaders to understand strategic requirements, manage expectations, and drive successful outcomes for critical initiatives.

* Drive the evaluation, pilot, and strategic adoption of new Microsoft 365 features, emerging technologies, and AI-powered capabilities (e.g., Copilot, Teams, Power Platform) to enhance productivity, collaboration, and user experience across the firm.

* Oversee the operational, performance, and incident management processes for Microsoft 365 environments, serving as a primary executive escalation point for critical issues and ensuring rapid resolution and root cause analysis.

Basic Qualifications

* 12-15+ years of progressive experience in enterprise-level IT, with at least 10 years specifically focused on Microsoft 365 platforms and services in a significant leadership capacity within a large, complex organization.

* Proven track record of successfully leading, managing, and developing large, geographically dispersed technical teams, including managers and individual contributors.

* Demonstrated ability to define, articulate, and execute complex technology strategies and

roadmaps for critical enterprise collaboration platforms.

* Deep architectural and engineering understanding of the entire Microsoft 365 suite, including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Teams, OneDrive, Azure AD, Intune, Purview, Windows 365, and the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI), as well as advanced security/compliance features.

* Extensive experience designing, implementing, and managing Microsoft 365 solutions within stringent security, governance, and regulatory frameworks, preferably in the financial services industry.

* Exceptional executive-level communication, presentation, and negotiation skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts and strategic initiatives to diverse audiences, including senior leadership and non-technical stakeholders.

* Superior analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities, capable of navigating highly complex technical, organizational, and business challenges.

Preferred Qualifications

* Hands-on experience with large-scale Microsoft 365 Copilot deployments, Power Platform integrations, custom integrations, and advanced automation strategies for M365 management and operations.

* Strong understanding of broader cloud architecture principles (Azure, AWS, GCP) and experience managing hybrid cloud environments.

* Relevant industry certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Microsoft 365 Certified: Enterprise Administrator Expert, or similar.

* Prior experience in a senior technology leadership role within a global financial institution, with a strong understanding of industry-specific challenges and regulations.

* Proficiency in advanced PowerShell scripting, Azure Automation, and other automation frameworks for M365 management and operations.

Salary Range The expected base salary for this Seattle, Washington, United States-based position is $150000-$250000. In addition, you may be eligible for a discretionary bonus if you are an active employee as of fiscal year-end.

Benefits Goldman Sachs is committed to providing our people with valuable and competitive benefits and wellness offerings, as it is a core part of providing a strong overall employee experience. A summary of these offerings, which are generally available to active, non-temporary, full-time and part-time US employees who work at least 20 hours per week, can be found here.

The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., 2018. All rights reserved Goldman Sachs is an equal employment/affirmative action employer Female/Minority/Disability/Vet.

Skills:

jobs.description:
Hello,

Greetings for the day!

My name is Yusuf, and I work as a Sr. Technical Recruiter at K-Tek Resourcing. We are looking for a Intune Engineer for one of our Fortune 500 clients based in the USA. We are searching for professionals for the business requirements listed below for one of our clients. Please review the requirements and connect with us if your profile suits them. Then send me an updated resume at or

Position: Intune Engineer

Location: Stamford, CT (Hybrid)

Employment Type: Contract

Key Skills:

· Need experience in supporting SCCM / Intune (WinOS, MacOS, ChromeOS) and VDI platforms (Citrix, AWS, Azure Virtual Desktop).

Responsibilities:

· Perform complex technical engineering tasks remotely or at customer site to meet business and contractual requirements

· Lead design and implementation of enterprise endpoint management solutions for Windows and macOS platforms

· Manage and deliver OS deployment solutions using Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (OSD, task sequences, imaging, packaging)

· Implement and support modern provisioning using Microsoft Intune (Autopilot, co-management, and compliance policies)

· Manage macOS device lifecycle using JAMF Pro

· Coordinate small technical teams and ensure delivery of work packages aligned with company processes

· Design and maintain Co-Management and Cloud Management Gateway (CMG) architectures

· Develop and maintain operational documentation, deployment standards, and best practices

· Ensure compliance with internal and customer procedures through reporting and analysis of completed work

· Provide technical leadership and guidance to junior engineers and support teams

· Perform root cause analysis (RCA) and produce detailed technical reports after incident resolution

· Support application deployment, packaging, and configuration management across enterprise endpoints

· Perform additional duties and special projects as required.

Experience & Requirements

· 8+ years of experience in enterprise endpoint engineering and large-scale environment support

Strong hands-on experience with:

· Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager (OSD, imaging, packaging, task sequences)

· Microsoft Intune (Autopilot, MDM, co-management)

· JAMF Pro (macOS management)

· Experience with WSUS, MDT, and patch management processes

· Strong understanding of operating system concepts, including Microsoft Active Directory, security, and OS internals

· Experience with SCCM Co-Management and Cloud Management Gateway (CMG)

· Strong knowledge of SQL and ability to write and troubleshoot queries for reporting and SCCM backend

· Experience with Microsoft cloud technologies such as Microsoft Azure and Microsoft 365

· Experience in mobile device management (MDM) and mobile application management (MAM)

· Strong scripting skills in PowerShell (preferred; Python also acceptable)

· Experience performing root cause analysis (RCA), incident troubleshooting, and detailed reporting

· Excellent debugging, analytical, and problem-solving skills

· Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

· Good understanding of the ITIL framework and service management processes.

Thanks and have a Great Day!!

jobs.description:
About the position

Pennant Services is seeking an experienced, Intune Engineer to design, implement, and support enterprise endpoint platforms. This role is responsible for the strategy, deployment, and lifecycle management of endpoint solutions including Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, Chromebooks, and virtual desktops (Azure Virtual Desktop).
